changed_construction_audit.plain_language_claim: The active label represented no current parser behavior: there was no constructor, accepted fixture, standardized case, or observed output. It was removed while the checked temporal-subordination source and future subtype question were preserved.

sources.citation: Yip, Ka-Fai. 2024. Two Types of Temporal Adverbial Clauses in Cantonese. WCCFL 39.
sources.locator: pp. 687-694; preserved source record in archived construction note

sources.citation: Canto Span constructor and output audit

implementation_validation: PASS required before handoff: 545 regression cases, 43 NP cases, 1,174 construction assertions, 169 note/test alignment, three constructor-specific zero-weight probes, low-reference wrapper audit, panel workflow, promotion and release gates, documentation links, and repository verification.
linguistic_confidence: No retained construction gained linguistic evidence or status. Three probes have zero evidence weight. TemporalAdverbialClause was retired because it encoded no implemented behavior; its checked source remains a future research record.
overclaim_review_scope: Inspected exact constructor calls and complete parser output for five low-reference labels. Distinguished internal direct-call fallback reachability from complete-parser output, avoided treating coherent probes as language evidence, and retired only the label with no constructor or output path.
unresolved_release_questions: Forty-nine labels still have no direct standardized case.
Comment remains constructor-level reachable but unobserved in complete parser output under the bounded single-lexeme topic scan.
The full dormant-label review remains due by handoff sequence 23.
RUL and PFV survey evidence remains blocked until the user supplies the survey-guidance prompt.
